mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate relative to recent months signals easing financial conditions, lowers the equity discount rate, and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not above baseline when FEDFUNDS declines for three consecutive monthly observations, or if triggered periods produce negative average returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE from its recent low indicates weakening household income growth and slower demand, which should depress equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if monthly unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is not negative over any 2-year sample window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both trend higher, firms are selling more while payrolls expand, implying stronger nominal demand and earnings momentum that lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint sales-plus-payroll expansion does not outperform either series alone, or if 60d forward returns are not positive in the top tercile of combined growth scores over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO without a matching rise in CPIAUCSL indicates real output gains without inflation pressure, a combination that should support equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL inflation over 3 months stays below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflationary industrial-production upswings are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than half of triggered observations, or if the conditional average return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals housing-demand weakness and a future slowdown in consumer spending, which should translate into weaker S&P 500 returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 8% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-contraction triggers are not followed by negative 20d SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if average triggered returns are positive over a rolling 24-month test window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPIAUCSL combined with an unchanged or higher FEDFUNDS rate tightens real rates and policy pressure, which should lower equity valuations and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS does not fall
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up, policy-not-easing episodes generate positive 60d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional return distribution is not below the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in PAYEMS while UNRATE stays flat or falls indicates improving labor-market breadth and income support, which should be bullish for equities over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for two consecutive months and UNRATE does not increase
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-expansion triggers fail to produce positive 5d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if returns are negative on average for any 18-month rolling subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock rebound, accelerating RSAFS and rising HOUST together indicate restored consumer demand and construction activity, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if combined sales-and-housing recoveries do not produce positive 60d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not above the unconditional average over a 3-year rolling window.
